title: daucus guttatus subsp. zahariadii
intro: Daucus guttatus subsp. zahariadii, also known as Zahariadi's carrot, is a subspecies of the wild carrot plant (Daucus carota), characterized by its unique leaf structure and growth habits. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Daucus guttatus subsp. zahariadii include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, relieve respiratory problems, and support the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to treat various skin conditions and wounds.
